_meta.method: 2025 FantasyPoints personnel mix (personnel_2026.json fp_personnel_mix / fp_heavy_rate_2025) = ground-truth baseline; 2026 direction projected from verified coordinator changes (coordinator_changes_2026.json), play-caller profiles (PLAYCALLER_PROFILES_2026.md / scheme_2026.json), and brain_intel.json quotes. Directions only — no invented 2026 percentages.
_meta.league_macro: vault macro: 'We're going from ~10 teams that felt comfortable in 12/13 personnel to nearly 2/3s of the league chasing it' (personnel_2026.json _meta); heavy usage is the league-wide 2026 trend, so 'hold' teams still sit inside a rising tide.

INFERENCE: the 13->12 rotation; the 12-up leg is directly sourced.

evidence.claim: Mike LaFleur keeps name dropping Tip Reiman, signaling more 12 personnel in 2026 for the team

with Drew Petzing, the Cardinals were 3rd in 13 personnel usage over the last two seasons — huge drop off to the 4th team (McBride, Reiman, Higgins)

projection_2026.rationale: The most explicit personnel quote in the brain: Commanders 'will use 12 Personnel a lot more — a lot closer to even vs 11 Personnel — they want to use it as the identity of their offense' (Chig Okonkwo + Ben Sinnott the pass-game keys), tied to Blough's Ben Johnson influence. From 30.6% heavy (12=20.2%), 12 rises sharply toward parity; more under-center/PA for Daniels supports it.

evidence.claim: Some good Commanders notes: Will use 12 Personnel a lot more — 'a lot closer to even' vs 11 Personnel — they want to use it as the identity of their offense (Chig + Ben Sinnott key in pass game)
Commanders likely lean more into 12 personnel (Ben Johnson influence)
Exciting changes in David Blough's scheme: Play action for Jayden. Moving Terry McLaurin around. Increased importance / stronger usage of Ben Sinnott
WAS arrivals include TE Chig Okonkwo (from TEN); Blough scheme = more under-center/balanced, less shotgun

WAS.notes: First-time caller = execution risk, but the stated personnel intent is unusually concrete.
